methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $60M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Seneca operates in the Drones & Autonomous sector at Series A stage, having raised $60M to date. The broader sector comprises 8 private peers (including Joby Aviation, Nuro, Archer Aviation, Elroy Air, and Rainmaker) and no public comparables. The narrow peer set—filtered by same stage, adjacent stage, and valuation tier—proved insufficient for robust median-based valuation, yielding fewer than three datapoints in each filtered category.

Given data constraints, Stage-Progression analysis was the primary applicable method. Using total raised ($60M) as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A to subsequent-stage progression, this method implies a $120M post-money valuation. This reflects standard investor expectations for capital deployment and progress metrics between funding rounds in early-stage frontier tech.

The triangulated range stands at $102M (low) to $138M (high), with a median of $120M. This range assumes execution of milestones consistent with Series A expectations and retention of current capitalization structure. Key uncertainties include: (i) achievement of technical and commercial milestones required to support next-round valuation step-up, (ii) the applicability of 2.0x multiplier assumptions to Seneca's specific market and competitive position, and (iii) lack of narrow peer data limiting cross-validation. A full valuation exercise would benefit from comparable company analysis once adjacent-stage peers emerge or public market comps materialize.
